original: Gregory Robertson does 200-mph free fall to save unconscious skydiver near Coolidge, Arizona
Superman first appears in DC Comics' Action Comics Series Issue #1, dated June 1938
Fossil evidence of the largest known snake 11-15 meters long named Vasuki indicus, 66 million years old, published from a mine in Panandhro, western India
Two earth-like planets are discovered orbiting the star Kepler-62
Cunard liner RMS Carpathia brings 705 survivors from the RMS Titanic to New York City
Paul Revere, William Dawes and Samuel Prescott ride from Charlestown to Lexington warning "the regulars are coming out!"
Longest game in Professional Baseball: Pawtucket Red Sox tie Rochester Red Wings 2-2 in 32 innings (game resumed 23rd June)
Wayne Gretzkyplays his last game in the NHL, as his New York Rangers lose 2-1 to the Pittsburgh Penguins
Portuguese superstarCristiano Ronaldobecomes first player to score 100 goals in the Champions League with a hat-trick in Real Madrid's 4-2 win over Bayern Munich
Bill Russellbecomes first African American coach in NBA history (Boston Celtics)
IBM produces 1st megabit-chip
London Bridge is sold to US oil company (to be erected in Arizona)
Jackie Robinsondebuts as 2nd baseman for the Montreal Royals
“Black Panther” is the first film shown at a commercial cinema in 35 years in Saudi Arabia as cinemas are reopened
Rick Moranis, Canadian actor (SCTV; Spaceballs; Ghostbusters; Honey I Shrunk the Kids), born in Toronto, Ontario
Hayley Mills, English actress (Parent Trap, Pollyanna), born in London, England
New report finds nearly half of China's cities are sinking due to development and extracting too much water
BBC news announcer announces "there is no news" at 20:45 news bulletin, plays music instead
NFL Draft: Tennessee quarterbackPeyton Manningfirst pick by Indianapolis Colts
Joan Benoit runs world record female marathon (2:22:43)
Pat Knauff, France sets 1-leg downhill ski speed record (115.012 mph)
LA Dodgers pitcherSandy Koufaxstrikes out the side on 9 pitches, for major league best third time in his career
Mike Schmidthits 500th home run (vs Robinson-Pirates)
John Stocktonbreaks his own NBA season assist record at 1,136
Kawhi Leonardwins the NBA Defensive Player of the Year for the second consecutive year
Stanley Cup Final, Maple Leaf Gardens, Toronto, ON: Toronto Maple Leafs beat Detroit Red Wings, 3-1 for a 4-3 series win; Toronto recovers after losing first 3 games

John Lennongives what becomes his final public performance at a gala salute to British media mogul Lew Grade at the Grand Ballroom of the Hilton Hotel, NYC; he performed 3 songs -Little Richard’s “Slippin’ and Slidin’”, Ben E. King’s “Stand By Me”, and closed with his own “Imagine”
"Wham!" become the first western pop act to release an album in China
Titan rocket explodes seconds after liftoff from Vandenberg AFB

George Washingtonissues General Orders announcing the end of hostilities with Britain in the American Revolutionary War, giving thanks to the Almighty, offering congratulations, and authorizing an extra ration of alcohol to the troops to celebrate
Paul Reveremeets withSamuel AdamsandJohn Hancockin Lexington, Massachusetts, and warns them that British troops are marching from Boston to Lexington
Natural History Museum opens in South Kensington, England
Dr James Campbell performed the 1st human nerve transplant
Kenny Ortega, American film and TV producer (High School Musical trilogy), born in Palo Alto, California
Jim Eisenreich, American MLB outfielder (Tourette syndrome, Florida Marlins WS 1997), born in St. Cloud, Minnesota
Jonathan Frid makes his 1st appearance as 200-year-old vampire Barnabas Collins on TV's "Dark Shadows", show becomes a huge hit
Whaling ship the Manhattan captained by Mercator Cooper is the first ship officially permitted visit Edo, Japan, in 220 years rescuing shipwrecked sailors
"Germantown Quaker Petition Against Slavery": Francis Daniel Pastorius presents 1st formal written protest against African-American slavery in English colonies in Germantown, Pennsylvania
Trial of Standing Bear-Crook on Native American citizenship rights begins
Sam Jethroe is 1st African American to play for Boston Braves
James McCune Smith, African-American doctor and abolitionist, born in Manhattan, New York (d. 1865)
Pulitzer Prize awarded toAlice Walkerfor the novel "The Color Purple"
Joan of Arcreceives beatification by the Roman Catholic Church at St. Peter's Basilica in the Vatican
Dutch Department of Amnesty International forms
World's Fair opens in Chicago
Stanley Cup Final, Maple Leaf Gardens, Toronto, ON: Toronto Maple Leafs go back-to-back with a 3-1 Game 5 win over Detroit Red Wings for a 4-1 series victory
Malcolm Marshall, West Indian cricket fast bowler (81 Tests, 376 wickets @ 20.94, BB 7/22; Barbados, Hampshire CCC, Natal), born in Bridgetown, Barbados (d. 1999)
Shirlie Holliman, English pop singer (Wham!, Pepsi & Shirlie), born in Bushey Heath, Hertfordshire, England
Wilber Marshall, American College Football HOF linebacker (Uni of Florida; Super Bowl 1985 Chicago Bears, 1991 Washington Redskins; 3 x Pro Bowl; 2 x All Pro), born in Titusville, Florida
Start of South Africa's 1st Test Cricket since 1970 (v WI Bridgetown)
Ivana Brlić-Mažuranić, Croatian writer (The Brave Adventures of Lapitch), born in Ogulin, Croatian Military Frontier, Austria-Hungary (d. 1938)
Leopold Stokowski, British conductor (Philadelphia Orchestra, 1912-40; Disney's Fantasia, 1939; American Symphony Orchestra, 1962-72), organist, and arranger, born in London, England (d. 1977)
George Shirley, American Grammy Award-winning tenor (first African-American tenor to perform a leading role at the Metropolitan Opera in New York City), and educator, born in Indianapolis, Indiana
Joseph L. Goldstein, American biochemist and physician (Nobel-1985), born in Kingstree, South Carolina
Jochen Rindt, Austrian auto racer (posthumous World F1 title 1970), born in Mainz, Germany (d. 1970)
Pan-Am Clipper begins regular passenger flights from San Francisco to Honolulu
Egypt & Israel agree to a cease fire
Zimbabwe (formerly Southern Rhodesia) declares independence from the United Kingdom
Bolesław I the Brave is crowned in Gniezno, becoming the first King of Poland
Battle of Fort Jackson, Fort St Philip & New Orlean's, Louisiana
